name: Aditxt, Inc.
business: Adimune’s proprietary immune modulation product, Apoptotic DNA Immunotherapy (ADI), utilizes a novel approach that mimics the way our bodies naturally induce tolerance to our own tissues. Pearsanta is pioneering the development of molecular tests based on the mitochondrial DNA to develop tests for early detection of cancer. AditxtScore is intended to detect individual immune responses to viruses, bacteria, peptides, drugs, supplements, bone marrow and solid organ transplants, and cancer.

exposures.sentence: Adimune’s proprietary immune modulation product, Apoptotic DNA Immunotherapy (ADI), utilizes a novel approach that mimics the way our bodies naturally induce tolerance to our own tissues.
AditxtScore is intended to detect individual immune responses to viruses, bacteria, peptides, drugs, supplements, bone marrow and solid organ transplants, and cancer.
Pearsanta is pioneering the development of molecular tests based on the mitochondrial DNA to develop tests for early detection of cancer.
On March 21, 2025, Pearsanta acquired certain patents related to the detection and analysis of DNA adducts.
Adivir is focused on building a portfolio of antiviral and other antimicrobial solutions designed to target life-threatening viral infections and emerging pathogens.

catalysts.event: Pre-IND meeting with FDA expected in Q2 2026 (implicitly)
catalysts.sentence: Adimune is working with the Mayo Clinic to prepare the IND package for FDA submission and is awaiting a pre-IND meeting expected in the second quarter of this year to review the package before full submission.

catalysts.event: Human trial for Stiff Person Syndrome expected to start in 2026
catalysts.sentence: Pending approval by the International Review Board and U.S. Food and Drug Administration, a human trial for SPS is expected to get underway in 2026 with enrollment of 10-20 patients.
The Company submitted its plan of compliance on January 15, 2026, and was granted an extension by Nasdaq until May 15, 2026, to regain compliance.

catalysts.event: Completion of Phase III clinical trials under LLU license
catalysts.sentence: the completion of Phase III clinical trials by March 31, 2026

catalysts.event: Biologic licensing approval (BLA) by FDA under LLU license
catalysts.sentence: biologic licensing approval (BLA) by the FDA by March 31, 2027

tamClaims.claim: Incidence of Stiff Person Syndrome is approximately one in one million individuals
tamClaims.sentence: According to the National Organization of Rare Diseases, the exact incidence and prevalence of SPS is unknown; however, one estimate places the incidence at approximately one in one million individuals in the general population.

Endometriosis occurs when the tissue of the uterus (endometrium) grows in areas where it does not belong, most often on the ovaries, fallopian tubes, outer surface of the uterus, and tissues holding the uterus, but can be found almost anywhere in the body. Endometriosis is challenging to identify, and on average takes ten years to diagnose, and when patients are finally diagnosed, greater than 90% have moderate to severe symptoms.
